title: The P.A.

jokes.text: Yeah, and those are big cups.

jokes.explanation: Clean double entendre with good economy, though somewhat predictable

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Yeah, and those are big cups.

jokes.text: I feel like I have 20 pounds of wet sand in my head.
jokes.punchline: Vivid, specific metaphor for mental fog
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific metaphor that perfectly captures Larry's neurotic self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I feel like I have 20 pounds of wet sand in my head.

jokes.text: I was nuttier in the late eighties.
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'nutty' as both crazy and peanut-related

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I was nuttier in the late eighties.

jokes.text: Hey, dad, where's the crapper? I gotta drop the kids off at the pool.
jokes.punchline: Crude bathroom euphemism in inappropriate context
jokes.explanation: Establishes Cully's crude character effectively through inappropriate timing

dialogue.speaker: Cully
dialogue.line: Hey, dad, where's the crapper? I gotta drop the kids off at the pool.

jokes.text: What a freak. What a big fucking freak.
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate brutal honesty once Cully's gone
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Larry's two-faced nature

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: What a freak. What a big fucking freak.

jokes.text: Talk up. They're in the crapper, for God's sakes.
jokes.punchline: Larry can't hear whispers, demands louder gossip

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Talk up. They're in the crapper, for God's sakes.

jokes.text: I was just thinking he's got such a great personality, maybe he should be the receptionist.
jokes.setup: Artie wants to hire crude Cully as PA
jokes.punchline: Larry sarcastically suggests even more visible position
jokes.explanation: Sharp sarcasm that reveals Larry's true feelings while staying polite

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I was just thinking he's got such a great personality, maybe he should be the receptionist.

jokes.text: You remember that summer we hired sheldon's son and we caught him in the xerox room with a copy of hustler? Remember what he was doing?
jokes.punchline: Implied masturbation with office equipment
jokes.explanation: Moderately effective cringe moment, but relies on cheap sex humor

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You remember that summer we hired sheldon's son and we caught him in the xerox room with a copy of hustler? Remember what he was doing?

jokes.text: What am I, a pussy? Why don't I send him something nice like a parasol and some earrings?
jokes.setup: Learning he gives thoughtful gifts to Artie
jokes.punchline: Overcompensating with stereotypically feminine items
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Larry's insecurity about his masculinity

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: What am I, a pussy? Why don't I send him something nice like a parasol and some earrings?

jokes.text: If you're within an 8-foot radius, whoop it up.
jokes.punchline: Specific measurement for fake enthusiasm zone
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specificity that perfectly captures the absurdity of showbiz ego-stroking

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: If you're within an 8-foot radius, whoop it up.

jokes.text: Don't lip off to the head rooster, you're set.
jokes.punchline: 'Head rooster' as nickname for Larry
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific metaphor that becomes running gag

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Don't lip off to the head rooster, you're set.

jokes.text: That's the kissing disease. Don't kiss me!
jokes.punchline: Immediately protective after stating medical fact
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy beat showing Larry's hypochondria

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: That's the kissing disease. Don't kiss me!

jokes.text: I got fruit baskets comin' out of my ass.
jokes.setup: Artie asking how PA job is going
jokes.punchline: Crude expression about mundane work
jokes.explanation: Consistent with Cully's crude character, effectively inappropriate

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I got fruit baskets comin' out of my ass.

jokes.text: Dad, that network dipshit is sitting in your office waiting for you.
jokes.punchline: Calling network executive 'dipshit' casually
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Cully's complete lack of workplace filter

dialogue.speaker: Cully
dialogue.line: Dad, that network dipshit is sitting in your office waiting for you.

jokes.text: It's not as thrilling as un-jamming the xerox machine, but no, I do not mind.
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comparison to equally mundane task
jokes.explanation: Good sarcasm with nice callback to earlier xerox reference

dialogue.speaker: Cully
dialogue.line: It's not as thrilling as un-jamming the xerox machine, but no, I do not mind.

jokes.text: Hey, slim pants, yogurt? No, thanks, fat pants.
jokes.punchline: Hank's immediate insult response to Cully's nickname
jokes.explanation: Quick verbal sparring that establishes tension between characters

dialogue.speaker: Cully
dialogue.line: Hey, slim pants, yogurt?

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: No, thanks, fat pants.

jokes.text: Non-dairy is strawberry swirl, or as I like to call it, the queer's delight.
jokes.punchline: Homophobic slur as casual flavor description
jokes.explanation: Effectively establishes Cully's offensive nature but relies on cheap shock value

dialogue.speaker: Cully
dialogue.line: Non-dairy is strawberry swirl, or as I like to call it, the queer's delight.

jokes.text: I'll have that. Lar will have queer's delight.
jokes.punchline: Larry uncomfortably adopts the offensive term
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Larry's awkward people-pleasing nature

dialogue.line: I'll have that.
Lar will have queer's delight.

jokes.text: I think he's very sexy, and I'd like to sleep with him.
jokes.punchline: Paula's blunt sexual interest
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Paula's directness contrasting with others' politeness

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: I think he's very sexy, and I'd like to sleep with him.

jokes.text: Good. I'm so tired of all the neurotic pussies around here.
jokes.punchline: Paula's criticism of the workplace dynamic
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation that perfectly captures the show's dynamic

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Good. I'm so tired of all the neurotic pussies around here.

jokes.text: Well, maybe he went to 'this can't be yogurt.' Where's that? It's right near 'what is this, yogurt?' You know, I don't care if he goes to 'hey, that's my asshole.' I just want some yogurt.
jokes.setup: Cully taking too long to get yogurt
jokes.punchline: Escalating to completely absurd fake store names
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating absurdist sequence that builds perfectly to the final punchline

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Well, maybe he went to 'this can't be yogurt.' Where's that? It's right near 'what is this, yogurt?' You know, I don't care if he goes to 'hey, that's my asshole.' I just want some yogurt.

jokes.text: Actually, I... I... I prefer it... extra-wet. It's, uh, more like real yogurt.
jokes.setup: Cully brings melted yogurt, Larry wants to complain
jokes.punchline: Larry awkwardly pretending to prefer the ruined product
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - his conflict avoidance leading to absurd rationalization

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Actually, I... I... I prefer it... extra-wet. It's, uh, more like real yogurt.

jokes.text: Everyone screws up the yogurt. I used to screw it up back when it was ice cream, and my car smelled like rotten milk, but that's show biz.
jokes.punchline: Artie's rambling justification and 'show biz' conclusion
jokes.explanation: Great Artie moment - his tendency to turn everything into showbiz wisdom

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Everyone screws up the yogurt. I used to screw it up back when it was ice cream, and my car smelled like rotten milk, but that's show biz.

jokes.text: You get to wear shorts, drive around with the door open. It's perfect in this fucking heat.
jokes.punchline: Practical reasons for wanting delivery job
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Cully's practical, anti-glamour mindset

dialogue.speaker: Cully
dialogue.line: You get to wear shorts, drive around with the door open. It's perfect in this fucking heat.

jokes.text: I noticed that the armrest was missing and the seats were very, uh, sticky.
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic description of car damage
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Larry's passive-aggressive complaint style

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I noticed that the armrest was missing and the seats were very, uh, sticky.

jokes.text: No one else has ever sat behind the wheel of my bentley except for the 3 previous owners and now... our cully.
jokes.punchline: Formal language contrasting with the situation
jokes.explanation: Good Larry passive-aggression disguised as formal politeness

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: No one else has ever sat behind the wheel of my bentley except for the 3 previous owners and now... our cully.

jokes.text: Yeah, especially since I drove my shitbox into the bus shelter.

dialogue.speaker: Cully
dialogue.line: Yeah, especially since I drove my shitbox into the bus shelter.

jokes.text: That b-12 shot's doing no good whatsoever. Actually, maybe I should get a shot in each cheek, because one half of my ass feels like it's alive and rarin' to go and the other half feels depressed... and lonely.
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizing his buttocks with emotional states
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist body humor with perfect Larry neurosis - creative and specific

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: That b-12 shot's doing no good whatsoever. Actually, maybe I should get a shot in each cheek, because one half of my ass feels like it's alive and rarin' to go and the other half feels depressed... and lonely.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Just look at kiefer sutherland.

jokes.text: Tell him I'm uncomfortable around new people... that you don't like anyone who's younger than you, that you're still mad about that 'queer's delight' crack.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Tell him I'm uncomfortable around new people... that you don't like anyone who's younger than you, that you're still mad about that 'queer's delight' crack.

jokes.text: With me gone, he'll have you all to himself.
jokes.setup: Cully figuring out he's being fired

jokes.explanation: Good insight into dysfunctional workplace relationship dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Cully
dialogue.line: With me gone, he'll have you all to himself.

jokes.text: Why, because cully hasn't learned how to kiss ass yet like everybody else around here?
jokes.punchline: Artie defending his son by criticizing workplace culture
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about showbiz sycophancy from protective father

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Why, because cully hasn't learned how to kiss ass yet like everybody else around here?

jokes.text: I just wanted my frozen yogurt frozen, that's all.
jokes.punchline: Larry's minimal, petty complaint
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry understatement - reducing complex workplace drama to petty complaint

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I just wanted my frozen yogurt frozen, that's all.

jokes.text: I can see this is a double Larry day.
jokes.setup: Meeting Larry Sanders with Larry King present
jokes.punchline: Noting the name coincidence
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor from guest appearance

dialogue.speaker: Larry King
dialogue.line: I can see this is a double Larry day.

jokes.text: In the name of the sweet baby Jesus, why am I so fat?
jokes.punchline: Religious exclamation about weight gain
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Larry's dramatic self-loathing

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: In the name of the sweet baby Jesus, why am I so fat?

jokes.text: Really? Thanks for telling me, because I wouldn't know what a magazine is. I'm a moron.
jokes.punchline: Cully's sarcastic self-deprecation
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response showing Cully's growing resentment

dialogue.speaker: Cully
dialogue.line: Really? Thanks for telling me, because I wouldn't know what a magazine is. I'm a moron.

jokes.text: Look, I'm sorry that you're a 50-year-old p.a., but that's not really my fault.
jokes.punchline: Brutal age-related insult
jokes.explanation: Devastating insult that perfectly captures Cully's cruel directness

dialogue.line: Look, I'm sorry that you're a 50-year-old p.a., but that's not really my fault.

jokes.text: Not after you stabbed the great one with a fork!
jokes.punchline: Revealing the extreme reason for the exclusion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reveal that explains everything - perfect escalation of family dysfunction

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Not after you stabbed the great one with a fork!

jokes.text: I was 6 years old. He was comin' at me.

dialogue.speaker: Cully
dialogue.line: I was 6 years old. He was comin' at me.

jokes.text: Parr, gleason, cavett, and now Larry. It's always the same. Larry, a guy who's so limp, he's like a fucking sock.
jokes.punchline: Brutal metaphor for Larry's personality
jokes.explanation: Devastating character assassination with perfect metaphor - captures Larry's essence

dialogue.speaker: Cully
dialogue.line: Parr, gleason, cavett, and now Larry. It's always the same. Larry, a guy who's so limp, he's like a fucking sock.

jokes.text: Don't you dare call Larry a sock!
jokes.punchline: Artie's passionate defense of the insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Artie's blind loyalty to Larry over his own son

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Don't you dare call Larry a sock!

jokes.text: Maybe he'll get that job at ups that he wanted.
jokes.punchline: Larry's attempt at consolation with callback
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Larry's awkward attempt at comfort

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Maybe he'll get that job at ups that he wanted.

jokes.text: I saw them my first day on the job. I saw the package. I said, 'let me take this to Larry personally.' I spaced. I left it in the trunk of the car.
jokes.punchline: Revealing Cully had them all along and forgot
jokes.explanation: Good callback that explains earlier mystery and shows Cully's carelessness

dialogue.speaker: Cully
dialogue.line: I saw them my first day on the job. I saw the package. I said, 'let me take this to Larry personally.' I spaced. I left it in the trunk of the car.

jokes.text: Isn't tampering with the mail a felony? Yeah, well, he's Artie's son.
jokes.setup: Cully keeping mail package for weeks
jokes.punchline: Larry's resigned acceptance of nepotism consequences
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending beat showing how nepotism corrupts everything, even basic law

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Isn't tampering with the mail a felony?

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, he's Artie's son.

tmdb_overview: Family ties are stretched to the limit when Artie hires his delinquent son as a production assistant. Larry worries about his diet.
